LIV-011.scenario: You are patching a lead vocal and stereo keyboard source into FOH while also building the main left/right system feed.
LIV-011.objective: Route each source to its intended stagebox input, then patch console main L/R outputs to the matching crossover inputs.

constraints.text: Sources must land on their assigned stagebox inputs rather than any open input.
Main L and Main R must stay on the matching left/right processor inputs.

LIV-011.completionExplanation: The completed patch puts vocal and stereo keys into FOH inputs and sends the console main L/R outputs into the system processor path in the correct left/right order.

LIV-015.scenario: You are creating a dedicated low-frequency, front-fill, or local zone feed while preserving the main PA path.
LIV-015.objective: Use the appropriate bus or matrix-style output for the dedicated zone processor, while keeping the main L/R processor feed separate.

constraints.text: The zone processor should receive the dedicated bus/matrix feed rather than a duplicated main PA output.

LIV-015.completionExplanation: The completed patch separates the main PA processor path from the dedicated zone/sub/front-fill processor feed.
LIV-015.migrationNotes: Promoted after source-route audit: launch data, roadmap intent, and renderer routes confirm a sub/matrix zone-feed board with Bus 2 to Sub Processor Input, Main L/R to system processor inputs, and Lead Vocal Mic to Stage Box Input 1. The renderer title still says Front Fill Zone Feed, but the route data is unambiguous and LIV-025 owns the dedicated front-fill board.

LIV-016.scenario: You are wiring a full stage input list into a front-of-house stagebox and preserving the main PA processor feed.
LIV-016.objective: Patch sources into the intended stagebox channel order and keep main L/R outputs routed to the correct processor inputs.

constraints.text: Input order matters; do not treat the stagebox as interchangeable open jacks.
The main PA processor path must keep left and right consistent.

LIV-016.completionExplanation: The completed patch keeps the stage input list in order and sends the stereo main mix into the PA processor path.
LIV-016.migrationNotes: Promoted after source-route audit: renderer, launch data, and LIV-016 repair handoff confirm the current board is 16-channel Stage Box to Front-of-House with 16 source-to-stagebox routes plus main L/R to crossover inputs. The older delay-tower roadmap entry is stale.

LIV-018.scenario: You are routing talkback so the production team can communicate with monitors without sending talkback to the audience PA.
LIV-018.objective: Patch talkback and related sources to their intended inputs and route the talkback output to the monitor/IEM destination only.

constraints.text: Talkback should feed the monitor path, not the audience-facing main PA.

LIV-018.completionExplanation: The completed patch keeps talkback in the monitor communication path while other sources land on their assigned FOH inputs.

LIV-019.scenario: You are assembling a compact show patch that combines drum inputs, five mono aux-to-IEM monitor feeds, stereo FX sends, and stereo FX returns.
LIV-019.objective: Patch the drum mics to stagebox inputs 1-8, route Aux 1-5 to IEM inputs 1-5, send Bus 1-4 to reverb and delay inputs in stereo pairs, and return both FX processors to FOH inputs 9-12.

constraints.text: Inputs, monitor sends, and FX returns serve different signal-flow roles.

LIV-019.completionExplanation: The completed patch keeps drum inputs on stagebox inputs 1-8, five mono aux monitor feeds on IEM inputs 1-5, stereo bus sends on the reverb and delay inputs, and stereo FX returns on FOH inputs 9-12.
LIV-019.migrationNotes: Corrected after source-route audit: LIV-019 uses 8 drum mic stagebox routes, five mono Aux-to-IEM monitor feeds, stereo reverb/delay sends, and stereo FX returns. It remains needs-review because the board has locked scroll, label, hitbox, stagebox, finalizer, and native cable behavior that must be preserved before any source manifest is created.

LIV-020.scenario: You are building a three-way main PA processor/amplifier chain while also feeding five aux monitor sends to IEM pack inputs.
LIV-020.objective: Patch Main L/R through the crossover, route high/mid/low crossover outputs through matching amplifiers to matching line-array inputs, and keep Aux 1-5 on the intended IEM pack input path.

constraints.text: Main PA outputs must pass through the processor/amplifier path before reaching speakers.
The five aux-to-IEM routes should not be confused with the audience PA chain.

LIV-020.completionExplanation: The completed patch keeps Main L/R feeding the crossover first, routes each high/mid/low crossover band through the matching left/right amplifier and line-array input, and keeps Aux 1-5 on the intended IEM pack inputs.
LIV-020.migrationNotes: Corrected after source-route audit: current LIV-020 is a locked Main PA + IEM Monitor Feed board with 14 stereo-grouped PA processor/amplifier/speaker routes and five mono Aux-to-IEM input routes. It remains needs-review because the board has locked gear, label, hitbox, false-jack, bad-route, and vertical layout behavior that must be preserved before any source manifest is created.

LIV-021.scenario: You are repairing a lead vocal chain that needs a correct mic input, channel insert compressor loop, PA feed, and monitor wedge feed.
LIV-021.objective: Patch the vocal mic input, complete the insert send/return through the compressor, and keep the monitor and main PA paths correct.

constraints.text: The insert send must feed the compressor input, and the compressor output must return to the insert return.
The monitor wedge and main PA are related outputs, but they are not interchangeable destinations.

LIV-021.completionExplanation: The completed patch restores the vocal input, places the compressor in the insert loop, and feeds both monitor and PA destinations through their intended outputs.

LIV-023.scenario: You are completing an advanced monitor-console patch with vocal and keyboard stage inputs, a vocal insert loop, a stereo IEM feed, and a multi-way PA system.
LIV-023.objective: Patch the vocal and keyboard inputs, complete the Channel 1 insert compressor loop, route the stereo IEM feed, and keep the main PA crossover and amplifier chain in correct left/right order.

constraints.text: Insert, monitor, and PA-processing routes belong to different subsystems and should not be collapsed into one cable-matching task.

LIV-023.completionExplanation: The completed patch lands the vocal and keyboard sources on the stagebox, completes the vocal compressor insert loop, feeds the stereo IEM transmitter from Aux 1 L/R, and routes Main L/R through the crossover to the matching high, mid, and low amplifier inputs.
LIV-023.migrationNotes: Corrected after source-route audit: current LIV-023 is a locked Monitor Console: Vocal Insert, Stereo IEM, and 3-Way PA board with 15 required routes, 30 good hitboxes, 101 false hitboxes, and six stereo groups. It remains needs-review because the board has custom scroll, legacy masking, gear, label, hitbox, false-jack, and broad invalid-route behavior that must be preserved before any source manifest is created.

LIV-026.scenario: You are routing a full-zone processing rig: Main L/R through system processing and crossover, Bus 1 to a delay tower chain, and Bus 2 to a front-fill chain.
LIV-026.objective: Patch Main L/R through the system processor and crossover to the high, mid, and low amp inputs, route Bus 1 through the delay tower processor to the stereo delay amp, and route Bus 2 through the front-fill processor to the fill amp.

constraints.text: Main L/R, Bus 1, and Bus 2 serve different zone-processing paths and should not be collapsed into one generic PA output.

LIV-026.completionExplanation: The completed patch sends Main L/R through system processing, crossover, and high/mid/low amplifier inputs; sends Bus 1 through delay processing to the stereo delay amp; and sends Bus 2 through front-fill processing to the fill amp.
LIV-026.migrationNotes: Corrected after source-route audit: current LIV-026 is the locked Full Zone Processing board with 15 required routes, 28 false hitboxes, baked true hitboxes, and six stereo groups across main/system, system/crossover, high/mid/low amp, and delay amp pairs. It remains reserved as needs-review until a manifest pass can preserve the custom scroll host, false-jack behavior, stack guard, processor-deco cleanup, broad invalid-route behavior, and existing route/checklist semantics.

LIV-028.scenario: You are solving a more complex talkback-to-monitor routing problem with several nearby stage and monitoring endpoints.
LIV-028.objective: Route talkback to the intended monitor/IEM destination while keeping source inputs and stereo monitoring paths in their correct roles.

constraints.text: Talkback belongs in the monitor communication path, not the main PA.
Monitor/IEM endpoints should be treated as monitor destinations, not general console inputs.

LIV-028.completionExplanation: The completed patch keeps talkback and monitoring routes isolated from audience-facing outputs while preserving source-input routing.
